Jakarta Persistence API v4.0.0-M2

    Deprecated API

    Contents

    • Terminally Deprecated
    • Enum Classes
    • Annotation Interfaces
    • Methods
    • Annotation Interface Elements
    • Terminally Deprecated Elements
      Element
      Description
      jakarta.persistence.EntityManager.createEntityGraph(String)
      Use EntityHandler.getEntityGraph(String) instead.
      jakarta.persistence.EntityManager.createQuery(CriteriaStatement<?>)
      Use EntityHandler.createStatement(CriteriaStatement).
      jakarta.persistence.Query.executeUpdate()
      Use Statement.execute().
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getCacheRetrieveMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getCacheRetrieveMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getCacheStoreMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getCacheStoreMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getFirstResult()
      Use TypedQuery.getFirstResult()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getLockMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getLockMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getMaxResults()
      Use TypedQuery.getMaxResults()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getResultList()
      This method returns a raw type. Use TypedQuery.getResultList() to execute queries.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getResultStream()
      This method returns a raw type. Use TypedQuery.getResultStream() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getSingleResult()
      Use TypedQuery.getSingleResult() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getSingleResultOrNull()
      Use TypedQuery.getSingleResult() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setCacheRetrieveMode(CacheRetrieveMode)
      Use TypedQuery.setCacheRetrieveMode(CacheRetrieveMode)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setCacheStoreMode(CacheStoreMode)
      Use TypedQuery.setCacheStoreMode(CacheStoreMode)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setFirstResult(int)
      Use TypedQuery.setFirstResult(int)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setLockMode(LockModeType)
      Use TypedQuery.setLockMode(LockModeType)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setMaxResults(int)
      Use TypedQuery.setMaxResults(int)
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.getResultList()
      This method returns a raw List. Use StoredProcedureQuery.getResultList(Class) instead.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.executeUpdate()
      This operation should never be called on a TypedQuery. Any DELETE or UPDATE query should be represented by an untyped instance of Query or, preferably, by an instance of Statement.
    • Deprecated Enum Classes
      Enum Class
      Description
      jakarta.persistence.TemporalType
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
    • Deprecated Annotation Interfaces
      Annotation Interface
      Description
      jakarta.persistence.MapKeyTemporal
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Temporal
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
    • Deprecated Methods
      Method
      Description
      jakarta.persistence.criteria.CriteriaQuery.multiselect(Selection<?>...)
      Since this method is not typesafe, the use of CriteriaBuilder.array(Selection...) or CriteriaBuilder.tuple(Selection...) with CriteriaQuery.select(Selection) is strongly preferred.
      jakarta.persistence.criteria.CriteriaQuery.multiselect(List<Selection<?>>)
      Since this method is not typesafe, the use of CriteriaBuilder.array(Selection...) or CriteriaBuilder.tuple(Selection...) with CriteriaQuery.select(Selection) is strongly preferred.
      jakarta.persistence.EntityManager.createEntityGraph(String)
      Use EntityHandler.getEntityGraph(String) instead.
      jakarta.persistence.EntityManager.createQuery(CriteriaStatement<?>)
      Use EntityHandler.createStatement(CriteriaStatement).
      jakarta.persistence.EntityManager.getDelegate()
      Use EntityHandler.unwrap(Class) instead.
      jakarta.persistence.MapKey.name()
      Use MapKey.value() instead
      jakarta.persistence.Query.executeUpdate()
      Use Statement.execute().
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getCacheRetrieveMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getCacheRetrieveMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getCacheStoreMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getCacheStoreMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getFirstResult()
      Use TypedQuery.getFirstResult()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getFlushMode()
      Use Query.getQueryFlushMode().
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getLockMode()
      Use TypedQuery.getLockMode()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getMaxResults()
      Use TypedQuery.getMaxResults()
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getResultList()
      This method returns a raw type. Use TypedQuery.getResultList() to execute queries.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getResultStream()
      This method returns a raw type. Use TypedQuery.getResultStream() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getSingleResult()
      Use TypedQuery.getSingleResult() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.getSingleResultOrNull()
      Use TypedQuery.getSingleResult() to execute queries
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setCacheRetrieveMode(CacheRetrieveMode)
      Use TypedQuery.setCacheRetrieveMode(CacheRetrieveMode)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setCacheStoreMode(CacheStoreMode)
      Use TypedQuery.setCacheStoreMode(CacheStoreMode)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setFirstResult(int)
      Use TypedQuery.setFirstResult(int)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setFlushMode(FlushModeType)
      Use Query.setQueryFlushMode(QueryFlushMode).
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setLockMode(LockModeType)
      Use TypedQuery.setLockMode(LockModeType)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setMaxResults(int)
      Use TypedQuery.setMaxResults(int)
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(int, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(int, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(Parameter<Calendar>, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(Parameter<Date>, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(String, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Query.setParameter(String, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setFlushMode(FlushModeType)
      Use Statement.setQueryFlushMode(QueryFlushMode).
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(int, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(int, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(Parameter<Calendar>, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(Parameter<Date>, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(String, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.Statement.setParameter(String, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.getResultList()
      This method returns a raw List. Use StoredProcedureQuery.getResultList(Class) instead.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setFlushMode(FlushModeType)
      Use StoredProcedureQuery.setQueryFlushMode(QueryFlushMode).
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(int, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(int, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(Parameter<Calendar>, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(Parameter<Date>, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(String, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.StoredProcedureQuery.setParameter(String, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.executeUpdate()
      This operation should never be called on a TypedQuery. Any DELETE or UPDATE query should be represented by an untyped instance of Query or, preferably, by an instance of Statement.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setFlushMode(FlushModeType)
      Use TypedQuery.setQueryFlushMode(QueryFlushMode).
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(int, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(int, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(Parameter<Calendar>, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(Parameter<Date>, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(String, Calendar, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
      jakarta.persistence.TypedQuery.setParameter(String, Date, TemporalType)
      Newly written code should use the date/time types defined in java.time.
    • Deprecated Annotation Interface Elements
      Annotation Interface Element
      Description
      jakarta.persistence.MapKey.name()
      Use MapKey.value() instead
